c.d: The method used to randomize the playback order of songs in a playlist. It affects how random the shuffle feels to the user and whether songs repeat frequently.
The ability of the app to efficiently manage and shuffle large playlists (1,000+ tracks) without performance issues or frequent repeats.
The capability to play downloaded music files without an internet connection.
The feature to mix personalized recommendations into the shuffled playlist based on the user's listening habits and preferences.
c.s: Spotify Support, Smart Shuffle Breathes New Life Into Your Spotify Playlists

c.d: How well the app organizes and manages the user's music library, including sorting, tagging, and playlist creation.
The design and ease of use of the app's interface.
The range of audio file formats supported by the app (e.g., MP3, FLAC, WAV, AAC).
Availability of a version of the app without advertisements.
